lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"DOM Manipulation is a key concept in web development that allows developers to interact with and dynamically change the structure, style, and content of a webpage using JavaScript. To fully grasp this concept, it's essential to understand what the DOM is and how JavaScript can be used to modify it. What is the DOM? The DOM (Document Object Model) is a tree-like structure that the browser creates when it loads an HTML document.","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: DOM Manipulation –\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"Each element in the HTML becomes a node in this tree. lessons.title: app.get('/users', (req, res) => { res.json(users);
lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"app.get('/users/:id', (req, res) => { const user = users.find(u => u.id === parseInt(req.params.id)); res.json(user); app.post('/users', (req, res) => { const newUser = { id: users.length + 1, name: req.body.name }; users.push(newUser); res.status(201).json(newUser); app.put('/users/:id', (req, res) => { const user = users.find(u => u.id === parseInt(req.params.id)); user.name = req.body.name; res.json(user); app.delete('/users/:id', (req, res) => { users = users.filter(u => u.id !== parseInt(req.params.id)); res.status(204).send(); app.listen(3000, () => console.log('API running on http://localhost:3000'));","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: app.get('/users', (req, res) => { res.json(users);\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"Mastering this concept will significantly improve your technical execution.","practiceTask":"Review app.get('/users', (req, res) => { res.json(users); and identify 3 real-world applications where this principle is used."}

lessons.title: Key Features of Mongoose
lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"Schema Definition A schema in Mongoose defines the structure of the documents in a collection. You can specify field types, default values, validation rules, and more. Example: const mongoose = require('mongoose'); const userSchema = new mongoose.Schema({ name: { type: String, required: true }, age: { type: Number, min: 0 }, email: { type: String, unique: true } This schema ensures that every user document has a name, age (non-negative), and a unique email.","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: Key Features of Mongoose\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"Mastering this concept will significantly improve your technical execution.","practiceTask":"Review Key Features of Mongoose and identify 3 real-world applications where this principle is used."}

lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"How CORS and Axios Work Together When using Axios to make requests from your frontend to a different domain or port, CORS must be properly configured on the server. Axios will send the request, but the browser will block the response unless the server’s CORS policy allows it. Also, if you're dealing with things like cookies or sessions, you need to configure Axios to send credentials: axios.defaults.withCredentials = true; and in your server: app.use(cors({ origin: 'http://localhost:3000', credentials: true })); In short: CORS protects users by preventing unauthorized cross-origin requests.","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: Information Technology Services\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"Axios simplifies making those requests from your frontend.
